description: Pre-computed rule excerpts for token-efficient loading

excerpts.coding-style:
CODING STYLE RULES (Core)

1. One responsibility per file - split when a file does more than one thing
2. Optimal file size: 200-400 lines. Hard max: 800 lines
3. Meaningful names - the name should reveal intent without a comment
4. No single-letter variables except: i, j, k for loop counters
5. Booleans: prefix with is, has, can, should (e.g., isValid, hasPermission)
6. Functions: verb + noun (calculateTotal, fetchUser, validateInput)
7. Constants: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (e.g., MAX_RETRIES, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS)
8. Prefer immutable by default: const, final, readonly
9. Early returns over nested if/else - flatten the happy path
10. Max 4 levels of nesting - extract to helper function if deeper
11. No magic numbers - every literal must have a named constant
12. Max 30 lines per function, max 5 parameters
13. Code should be self-documenting - comments explain WHY
14. Consistent style enforced by formatter (Prettier, Black, gofmt)
excerpts.security:
SECURITY RULES (Critical)

1. NEVER hardcode secrets, API keys, passwords, tokens
2. Use environment variables or secrets manager (Vault, AWS SSM)
3. .env files MUST be in .gitignore - never committed
4. Rotate secrets on suspected exposure - assume breach
5. ALWAYS validate/sanitize user input at every system boundary
6. Use allowlists over denylists (define what IS valid)
7. Use parameterized queries - NEVER concatenate SQL strings
8. Validate file paths to prevent path traversal (../../../etc/passwd)
9. Escape all output to prevent XSS (HTML-encode, JS-encode)
10. Hash passwords with bcrypt (cost 12+) or argon2id - NEVER MD5/SHA1
11. Session tokens: cryptographically random, min 128 bits entropy
12. JWT: validate signature, check exp, iss, aud - reject alg: none
13. Use httpOnly, secure, sameSite=Lax for session cookies
14. HTTPS only - redirect HTTP, use HSTS header
15. Rate limiting on all public endpoints
excerpts.database:
DATABASE RULES (Core)

1. ALWAYS use parameterized queries - never concatenate user input
2. Every table must have a primary key - prefer UUID over auto-increment
3. Add created_at and updated_at timestamps to every table
4. Use foreign keys to enforce referential integrity
5. Index foreign keys - always, no exceptions
6. Index columns used in WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY clauses
7. Use composite indexes for multi-column queries
8. Avoid N+1 queries - use JOINs or batch loading (dataloader)
9. Use connection pooling - never open/close per request
10. Set connection timeout (5s) and idle timeout (300s)
11. Profile queries exceeding 100ms
12. Never expose raw database errors to end users
13. Use bulk inserts for batch operations
14. Keep transactions short - no external API calls inside
15. Backup daily, test restore monthly
excerpts.testing:
TESTING RULES (Core)

1. Write tests BEFORE or WITH implementation (TDD preferred)
2. Each test tests ONE thing - one assertion per logical concept
3. Follow Arrange/Act/Assert (AAA) pattern
4. Keep tests short: 10-20 lines max (excluding setup)
5. Test names must describe scenario and expected outcome
6. Format: test_<what>_<condition>_<expected>
7. Always test: null/empty/boundary values, error paths
8. Mock external dependencies: APIs, databases, file system, time
9. Each test runs independently - no shared mutable state
10. Tests MUST pass before merge - no exceptions
11. Minimum 80% coverage for new code, 60% for legacy
12. Flaky tests are bugs - fix or delete them
13. Test suite should complete in under 5 minutes
14. Use pytest fixtures over setUp/tearDown
15. Use pytest.mark.parametrize for data-driven tests
excerpts.api:
API DESIGN RULES (Core)

1. Use plural nouns for resources: /users, /orders (not /user)
2. Use kebab-case for multi-word: /order-items (not /orderItems)
3. Nest resources max 2 levels deep
4. Use query parameters for filtering, sorting, pagination
5. Version in URL path: /api/v1/users (not headers)
6. GET: read-only, idempotent, cacheable - never modify state
7. POST: create new resources, return 201 + Location header
8. PUT: full resource replacement, idempotent
9. PATCH: partial update, send only changed fields
10. DELETE: remove resource, return 204 No Content
11. Return 400 for invalid input, 401 for auth missing, 403 for forbidden
12. JSON by default - use Content-Type: application/json
13. Dates in ISO 8601 format: 2026-03-07T14:30:00Z
14. Large collections: always paginate with limit/offset or cursor
15. Rate limit by API key + IP
excerpts.git:
GIT WORKFLOW RULES (Core)

1. Use Conventional Commits: type(scope): description
2. Types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore, perf, ci
3. Description: imperative mood, lowercase, no period at end
4. Body: explain WHY, not what (the diff shows what)
5. Max 72 chars for subject line, wrap body at 80 chars
6. One logical change per commit - don't mix features and fixes
7. Small, atomic commits that can be reverted independently
8. Branch format: type/ticket-short-description (kebab-case)
9. main: always deployable, protected
10. Delete branches after merge
11. NEVER commit: .env, credentials, node_modules/, __pycache__/
12. Keep PRs small: <400 lines changed
13. PR description must explain: WHY, HOW TO TEST, BREAKING CHANGES
14. Request review from at least 1 person
15. Address all review comments before merge
excerpts.python:
PYTHON PATTERNS (Core)

1. Follow PEP 8, use Black for formatting, ruff for linting
2. Type hints mandatory for all function signatures (params + return)
3. Use from __future__ import annotations for modern syntax (3.9+)
4. Use dataclasses for simple data containers
5. Use Pydantic for data with validation (API input/output, configs)
6. Use pathlib.Path over os.path for all path operations
7. Context managers (with) for ALL file operations - never f = open()
8. Use async/await for I/O-bound operations (HTTP, DB, file I/O)
9. Catch specific exceptions, never bare except: or except Exception:
10. Use logging module - never print() in production code
11. Generators over lists for large/unbounded sequences
12. Use functools.lru_cache for expensive pure functions
13. Use pytest as test framework, fixtures over setUp/tearDown
14. Virtual environments mandatory: venv, poetry, uv, or conda
15. Minimum Python version: 3.10+ for new projects
excerpts.typescript:
TYPESCRIPT PATTERNS (Core)

1. strict: true in tsconfig.json - always, no exceptions
2. Avoid any - use unknown with type guards, or define actual type
3. Interfaces for object shapes, type aliases for unions/intersections
4. Use discriminated unions for state machines and variant types
5. Use readonly for immutable properties, ReadonlyArray<T> for arrays
6. Use zod or valibot for runtime validation of external data
7. Named exports only - no default exports (better refactoring)
8. Always handle Promise rejections (.catch() or try/catch with await)
9. Prefer undefined over null (TypeScript convention)
10. Use satisfies for compile-time type checking without widening
11. Use Result types for expected failures: { ok: true, value } | { ok: false, error }
12. Validate at system boundaries: API handlers, env vars, configs
13. Use ESLint with @typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked
14. Use vitest or jest with ts-jest for testing
15. Barrel exports (index.ts) only at module boundaries
excerpts.go:
GO PATTERNS (Core)

1. Always check returned errors - never use _ to discard
2. Wrap errors with context: fmt.Errorf('context: %w', err)
3. Use errors.Is() and errors.As() for comparison - never string matching
4. Define interfaces at consumption site, not definition site
5. Keep interfaces small: 1-3 methods (bigger = weaker abstraction)
6. Accept interfaces, return structs
7. First parameter: ctx context.Context for I/O or cancellable functions
8. Never store context in structs - pass it through calls
9. Prefer channels for communication, mutexes for state protection
10. Channel direction in signatures: chan<- (send), <-chan (receive)
11. Use sync.WaitGroup for waiting on multiple goroutines
12. Use table-driven tests as default pattern
13. Test helpers: use t.Helper() for correct failure line reporting
14. Short variable names for small scopes, longer for larger scopes
15. Use go vet + staticcheck + golangci-lint in CI
